commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8db9dce190a00fa927c2bb71b0c4c011599e2749)
commands.review_summary:
The PR deletes `public/CNAME` and updates retired trust threat-model pages to add noopener/noreferrer on external links and simplify Korean detail label wiring.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence current-main reproduction exists for the trust-page link/detail-label changes because those pages were retired. The CNAME concern is source-visible because current main still contains `public/CNAME` with `clawd.bot`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer needs to decide the hosting/CNAME direction and the contributor needs to rebase/prove the remaining behavior before a repair lane would be safe.

target.title: fix(trust): add rel noopener to external links, remove dead CNAME, align ko detailLabels
target.body:
Three trust page hygiene fixes from a security audit.

## Changes

1. **Add `rel="noopener noreferrer"` to all external links** (21 HTML + 4 JS `setAttribute` calls across en/ko/ja/zh-cn trust pages). Without this, `target="_blank"` links give the opened page access to `window.opener`, which is a known phishing vector (reverse tabnabbing).

2. **Remove `public/CNAME`** containing `clawd.bot`. This is a GitHub Pages artifact from before the site moved to Vercel. Vercel ignores it, but it is served as a static text file at `/CNAME`, leaking an old domain name.

3. **Align ko threatmodel `detailLabels` with ja/zh-cn shorthand**. The Korean page manually destructured all 7 properties from `t.detail` into a new object literal, while ja and zh-cn both use the clean `detailLabels: t.detail` shorthand. All three locale data files have identical `detail` object shapes, so the manual destructuring is pure redundancy that diverges from the established pattern.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trust page external links missing noopener, dead CNAME served as static file, ko locale code divergence from ja/zh-cn
Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, git diff verification
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
# Verify all target="_blank" links have rel="noopener noreferrer"
grep -c 'target="_blank"' src/pages/trust/**/threatmodel.astro
grep -c 'rel="noopener noreferrer"' src/pages/trust/**/threatmodel.astro
# Verify CNAME removed
ls public/CNAME 2>&1  # "No such file or directory"
# Verify ko uses same shorthand as ja/zh-cn
grep 'detailLabels' src/pages/trust/ko/threatmodel.astro
grep 'detailLabels' src/pages/trust/ja/threatmodel.astro
```
Evidence after fix: all 21 HTML links and 4 JS setAttribute calls include `rel="noopener noreferrer"`; CNAME deleted; ko uses `detailLabels: t.detail` matching ja/zh-cn
Observed result after fix: `git diff --check` clean, no whitespace issues
What was not tested: live Vercel deployment (requires deploy preview)
target.author: sebtardif
